analysis.consistency: High. The spell components, duration, and effects are clearly tied to the chosen version of imprisonment. The general rules (no aging, breathing, divination immunity) apply universally before the version-specific effects take hold.
analysis.quality: Excellent. This is a well-detailed 9th-level spell. The inclusion of multiple distinct imprisonment options provides significant utility and thematic variety. The rules for ending the spell (custom condition, high-level dispel magic targeting the component) are appropriate for a spell of this magnitude.

mechanics.saving_throw: Wisdom (standard for effects targeting the mind/will).
mechanics.spell_save_dc_interaction: Target that succeeds is immune to subsequent castings by the same caster, which is an important balancing factor against repeated attempts.
mechanics.component_cost: The 500 gp per Hit Die requirement is a significant gold sink, appropriate for a high-level, long-duration incapacitation spell. It scales appropriately with the target's power.

sub-options.name: Burial
sub-options.effect_summary: Entombed in a magically sealed sphere, immune to teleportation/planar travel.
sub-options.counterplay_notes: Strong isolation, immune to most relocation spells.

sub-options.name: Chaining
sub-options.effect_summary: Target is Restrained and cannot be moved.
sub-options.counterplay_notes: Less total isolation than Burial, but keeps the target physically present.

sub-options.name: Hedged Prison
sub-options.effect_summary: Transported to a private demiplane (labyrinth, cage, etc.), blocked against teleportation/planar travel.
sub-options.counterplay_notes: Excellent for removing a threat from the material plane without killing it, relies on the caster defining a navigable/explorable space.

sub-options.name: Minimus Containment
sub-options.effect_summary: Shrinks target to 1 inch, contained in a nigh-indestructible gemstone. Target remains visible.
sub-options.counterplay_notes: Unique visual and physical restriction. Visibility means allies can potentially know where the target is, but interaction is impossible.

sub-options.name: Slumber
sub-options.effect_summary: Target falls asleep and cannot be awakened.
sub-options.counterplay_notes: The simplest effect, relying solely on the target failing the save. Less robust containment than the others, but the 'can't be awoken' clause is key.

mechanics.ending_conditions: The custom condition mechanic is flexible and prevents permanent trivialization by 9th-level casters, requiring creative and reasonable goal-setting agreed upon by the GM.
